content.problemStatement:
Stack likes the number 333 a lot.

He has two non-negative integers AAA and BBB.

In one operation, Stack can do either of the following:


A:=ŌłŻAŌłÆBŌłŻA:=|A-B|A:=ŌłŻAŌłÆBŌłŻ (change AAA to ŌłŻAŌłÆBŌłŻ|A-B|ŌłŻAŌłÆBŌłŻ)
B:=ŌłŻAŌłÆBŌłŻB:=|A-B|B:=ŌłŻAŌłÆBŌłŻ (change BBB to ŌłŻAŌłÆBŌłŻ|A-B|ŌłŻAŌłÆBŌłŻ)
Note that ŌłŻXŌłŻ|X|ŌłŻXŌłŻ denotes absolute value of XXX. For example ŌłŻŌłÆ7ŌłŻ=7|-7| = 7ŌłŻŌłÆ7ŌłŻ=7 and ŌłŻ4ŌłŻ=4|4| = 4ŌłŻ4ŌłŻ=4.

Find the **minimum** number of operations after which **at least** one integer out of AAA and BBB becomes divisible by 333.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The only line of each test case contains two integers AAA and BBB.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the **minimum** number of operations after which **at least** one integer out of AAA and BBB becomes divisible by 333.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: A=0A=0A=0 is already divisible by 333.
Test case 222: In the only operation, Stack can change A=1A=1A=1 to A=ŌłŻAŌłÆBŌłŻ=ŌłŻ1ŌłÆ1ŌłŻ=0A = |A-B| = |1-1| = 0A=ŌłŻAŌłÆBŌłŻ=ŌłŻ1ŌłÆ1ŌłŻ=0. Now A=0A=0A=0 is divisible by 333.
